cmad.notes: No CMAD located for PROBA2. PROBA2 is an ESA technology-demonstration mission (launched 2009-11-02) and is not subject to the NASA HQ CMAD requirement. A web search for 'PROBA2 Calibration and Measurement Algorithms Document' returned only CMADs for other missions (STEREO/SECCHI, STEREO/WAVES, MMS). Current-team evidence came instead from the official ESA PROBA2 mission team page, the four PROBA2 instrument SPASE records, and the SWAP/LYRA/mission description papers.

evidence.source: Observatory record Contacts (Principal Investigator)
Official ESA PROBA2 mission team page (Science Coordinator/Operator, ESA ESTEC)
Co-author, mission overview paper 'PROBA2: Mission and Spacecraft Overview' (2013SoPh..286....5S)
Principal Investigator in the PROBA2/SWAP Instrument SPASE record
Official ESA PROBA2 mission team page (SWAP Scientific Principal Investigator, Royal Observatory of Belgium)
2nd author of the SWAP instrument overview paper (Seaton et al. 2013 — non-alphabetized)

Recorded for downstream awareness: for a mission-level Observatory record the role agent may still want him as a ProjectManager/MissionManager Contact.

notes: This is the mission-level Observatory record. Its own Contacts name only Joe Zender (Principal Investigator) and its only link is the ESA PROBA2 website (http://sci.esa.int/proba2/). It carries NO InstrumentIDs, OperatingSpan, RevisionHistory, or PublicationInfo. The four instrument records were reached via the SMWG Instrument registry (https://spase-metadata.org/SMWG/Instrument/PROBA2/): SWAP, LYRA, DSLP, TPMU. Consider adding these InstrumentIDs to the Observatory record downstream.

The Observatory Description mentions five experiments (SWAP, LYRA, TPMU, DSLP, and an SGVM vector magnetometer), but only four have SPASE Instrument records and appear on the ESA team page (SWAP, LYRA, DSLP, TPMU). No SGVM SPASE Instrument record or SGVM PI was found.

The official ESA PROBA2 mission team page (https://sci.esa.int/web/proba2/48482-mission-team) is the most authoritative provider source and names all six instrument PIs (scientific + technical): Berghmans (SWAP Sci PI) & Defise (SWAP Tech PI); Dominique (LYRA PI) & Schmutz (LYRA Tech PI); Travenicek (DSLP PI); Hruska (TPMU PI). All are Strong. Instrument PIs are also confirmed in their instrument SPASE records where those exist (SWAP, LYRA, DSLP, TPMU). The downstream role agent should decide whether instrument PIs belong on the Observatory record or only on their respective Instrument records.

Description papers used: SWAP Part I (Seaton et al. 2013, arXiv:1208.4631 — non-alphabetized, first 5 authors Seaton, Berghmans, Nicula, Halain, De Groof); LYRA description paper (Dominique et al. 2013, doi:10.1007/s11207-013-0252-5); and the mission/spacecraft overview (Santandrea et al. 2013, 2013SoPh..286....5S, doi:10.1007/s11207-013-0289-5; author order Santandrea, Gantois, Strauch, Teston, Tilmans, Baijot, Gerrits, De Groof, Schwehm, Zender). Daniel B. Seaton is included at Medium as the SWAP paper lead author (his team-page role is only 'SWAP instrument scientist').

Consistency-with-repo judgment: the ESA mission overview paper is a spacecraft/engineering overview, and its lead/near-lead authors (Santandrea, Gantois) plus the ESA managers (Schwehm Mission Manager, Strauch Project Manager) are engineering/management personnel. Following this repo's SOHO precedent (Project Manager Kevin Schenk excluded; overview lead-author Domingo excluded), these four are recorded as EXCLUDED rather than included as data authors. NOTE: a prior run of this file had included them at Medium; they are demoted here for consistency. For a mission-level Observatory record the downstream role agent may still legitimately want the ESA mission/project managers as ProjectManager/MissionManager Contacts — hence they are retained in the list with reasons rather than dropped.
'P. Travenicek' on the ESA team page matches the DSLP Instrument SPASE record spelling 'Pavel M. Travenicek' (Person record Pavel.M.Travenicek exists), resolving the prior run's spelling ambiguity.

Person records confirmed present: Joe.Zender, David.Berghmans, Marie.Dominique, Pavel.M.Travenicek, Frantisek.Hruska, Anik.De.Groof, Daniel.B.Seaton (ORCID 0000-0002-0494-2025). Person records NOT found (HTTP 404): Jean-Marc.Defise, Werner.Schmutz, and all excluded ESA/support personnel. Downstream should create Person records for Defise and Schmutz (Strong instrument-PI candidates) if promoted.

Support/operations/student staff on the ESA team page not listed individually as they are clearly below the authorship bar: E. D'Huys (Ph.D. student), S. Yalim (researcher), S. Willems (system engineer), E. Tilmans (Spacecraft Operations Manager, Redu), and P. Reis / F. Hannoteau / C. Baijot (Spacecraft Operations Engineers, Redu). Overview-paper co-authors F. Teston and D. Gerrits are likewise below the bar (non-lead, engineering/operations).

Crossref returned no ORCIDs or affiliations for the overview paper; affiliations shown come from the ESA team page and the SPASE Person/Instrument records. ORCIDs were captured opportunistically only (Seaton from his Person record).
